title: 11 Soothing Herbal Creams to Help Infants with Pertussis

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of pertussis, also known as whooping cough. This herbal remedy contains properties like alkylamides and caffeic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects. These constituents can help reduce the severity of coughing fits and ease congestion. The creams can also boost the immune system by stimulating the production of white blood cells, helping the body fight off the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes the infection. To use Echinacea purpurea creams effectively, apply a small amount to the affected areas, such as the chest, throat, and nose, 2-3 times a day. You can also mix a few drops of the cream with a carrier oil, like coconut or olive oil, and apply it to the skin after a warm bath to help the cream absorb better. It's essential to note that Echinacea purpurea creams are not a substitute for medical treatment. If you or a family member is diagnosed with pertussis, consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment. When using Echinacea purpurea creams, you may notice a reduction in the frequency and severity of coughing fits within a few days. The cream may also help alleviate congestion and sinus pressure, making it easier to breathe. However, it's crucial to continue with any prescribed medication and follow the recommended treatment plan to ensure the infection is fully cleared.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for pertussis

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contain properties that may help alleviate symptoms of pertussis, also known as whooping cough. This is due to the presence of eucalyptol, a compound that has natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory effects. Eucalyptol helps to loosen mucus in the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing coughing fits. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties may help soothe irritated tissues in the throat and chest. If you or your child is experiencing symptoms of pertussis, you can try applying Eucalyptus globulus cream to the chest and throat area to see if it provides relief. Gently massage the cream into the skin, and you can also inhale the vapors from a warm compress or a bowl of hot water with the cream added to it. However, it is essential to note that Eucalyptus globulus creams are not a substitute for medical treatment. If you suspect pertussis, consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment. Using Eucalyptus globulus creams in conjunction with medical treatment may help to reduce the severity of symptoms and the duration of the illness. You may notice a decrease in coughing fits, improved breathing, and reduced fever. However, it is crucial to follow the advice of a healthcare professional and not rely solely on Eucalyptus globulus creams for treatment. As with any new treatment, monitor your body's response and adjust accordingly.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for pertussis

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams, also known as ginger creams, have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of pertussis, also known as whooping cough. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that may help reduce the severity of the illness. These compounds can help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, and also have a soothing effect on the throat and chest. Additionally, ginger has natural expectorant properties, which can help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways.  To use Zingiber officinale creams for pertussis, you can apply a thin layer to the affected area, such as the chest or throat, and gently massage it in. You can also take a warm bath with ginger-infused water or apply a ginger compress to the chest. It is essential to note that while ginger creams may provide some relief, they should not be used as a replacement for medical treatment. If you or your child is experiencing symptoms of pertussis, you should consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.  When using Zingiber officinale creams for pertussis, you may experience a reduction in coughing and a decrease in the thickness of mucus. You may also notice an improvement in your overall well-being and a decrease in the severity of symptoms such as fever and fatigue. However, it is crucial to follow the advice of your healthcare professional and use the cream as directed.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for pertussis

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia creams are used to help with pertussis, also known as whooping cough, a contagious respiratory infection. The creams are made from the root of the Echinacea angustifolia plant, which contains properties like alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and phenylethanoid glycosides. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects that may help reduce symptoms like coughing, fever, and congestion. Alkylamides, in particular, have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help boost the body's immune response to fight off the infection.  To use Echinacea angustifolia creams for pertussis, you can apply a small amount to the affected area, such as the chest or nose, 2-3 times a day. It's essential to note that creams may not be as effective as other forms of Echinacea, like capsules or teas, and they should not be considered a substitute for medical treatment. Always consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you're pregnant, breastfeeding, or taking medications. If you're experiencing severe symptoms, difficulty breathing, or high fever, seek medical attention immediately.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea angustifolia herbal creams for pertussis

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams, also known as thyme creams, have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of pertussis, also known as whooping cough. This is because thyme contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol, which have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes the infection, reducing the severity of symptoms like coughing and fever. When using Thymus vulgaris creams for pertussis, take the following steps: apply a small amount of the cream to the affected area, such as the chest or throat, 2-3 times a day; gently massage it in until absorbed; and, if necessary, repeat the process for children over the age of 2, or as directed by a healthcare professional. As a result of using the cream, you may experience a reduction in coughing and fever, making it easier to breathe and relax. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe a sore throat and reduce discomfort. However, it's essential to note that Thymus vulgaris creams are not a replacement for medical treatment, and you should consult a healthcare professional before using it, especially if you have a severe case of pertussis or are experiencing difficulty breathing.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for pertussis

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams, also known as peppermint creams, may help alleviate symptoms of pertussis (whooping cough) due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The creams contain menthol and menthone, which are volatile oils that can help reduce coughing and relieve congestion. They also contain flavonoids, such as limonene and beta-pinene, which have antitussive properties, helping to suppress coughing.  To use Mentha x piperita creams for pertussis, apply a small amount to your chest or nose before each coughing episode. You can also inhale the vapors from the creams to help ease congestion. However, be sure to consult with a healthcare professional before using the creams, especially if you are pregnant, breastfeeding, or have a history of allergies. Taking action with Mentha x piperita creams may lead to a reduction in coughing frequency and severity, improved sleep quality, and increased comfort. You may also experience a decrease in the number of coughing fits, allowing you to manage your symptoms more effectively. However, it is essential to continue following the treatment plan recommended by your healthcare provider, as the creams are not a replacement for medical treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for pertussis

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus creams may help alleviate symptoms of pertussis, also known as whooping cough, due to its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties. This herb contains various constituents such as astragalosides, flavonoids, and polysaccharides, which work together to boost the immune system and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. To use Astragalus membranaceus creams for pertussis, apply a thin layer to the chest and back area 2-3 times a day. It's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you're already taking medication or have a weakened immune system.  When applied topically, Astragalus membranaceus creams may help to: - Reduce coughing and wheezing - Soothe inflammation and irritation in the respiratory tract - Improve sleep quality by reducing night-time coughing episodes - Shorten the duration of the illness However, it's crucial to note that Astragalus membranaceus creams may not completely eliminate the symptoms of pertussis. If you experience severe symptoms, such as difficulty breathing, chest pain, or a fever over 102°F (39°C), seek medical attention immediately.
remedies.image_alt: Astragalus membranaceus herbal creams for pertussis

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams, also known as tea tree oil creams, have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of pertussis, a highly contagious respiratory illness. The active compound in these creams, terpinen-4-ol, has antimicrobial properties that help combat the Bordetella pertussis bacteria, which causes the illness. Tea tree oil also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce coughing fits and soothe a sore throat. To use Melaleuca alternifolia creams for pertussis, apply a thin layer to the affected area (such as the chest or throat) 2-3 times a day. You can also mix a few drops of tea tree oil with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil and apply it to the affected area. It's essential to note that while these creams can help alleviate symptoms, they are not a substitute for medical treatment. If you suspect you or your child has pertussis, consult a doctor immediately. As the bacteria are eliminated and the body recovers, symptoms such as coughing and sneezing should decrease, and fever should subside. However, it's crucial to continue following medical treatment as prescribed by your doctor to ensure the illness is fully cleared.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for pertussis

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus creams have been traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of pertussis, also known as whooping cough. The creams contain mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that help soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat and chest. The main constituents of Verbascum thapsus creams are isosteroids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce coughing and fever. When using Verbascum thapsus creams for pertussis, it's recommended to apply a thin layer to the affected areas, ideally 2-3 times a day, and to mix it with a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil to make it more easily absorbed.  As the creams work, you may start to notice a decrease in coughing and a reduction in fever. The mucilages and isosteroids may help protect the mucous membranes from further irritation, allowing them to heal and recover more quickly. However, it's essential to note that Verbascum thapsus creams are not a substitute for medical treatment. If you suspect you or someone else has pertussis, it's crucial to seek medical attention for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Verbascum thapsus herbal creams for pertussis

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams, also known as licorice root creams, have been studied for their potential in treating pertussis, a highly contagious respiratory illness. The creams contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the severity of pertussis symptoms such as coughing and sore throats. Glycyrrhizin also has expectorant properties, meaning it can help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe. Additionally, the creams contain other constituents like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial properties that can help fight off the Bordetella pertussis bacteria that causes the illness. If you suspect you or a family member has pertussis, you can try using Glycyrrhiza glabra creams as part of a treatment plan. Apply the creams topically to the affected areas, such as the chest and throat, 2-3 times a day, and use them in conjunction with other treatments such as antibiotics and cough suppressants. By taking these steps, you may experience a reduction in coughing fits and an improvement in overall comfort levels. However, it's essential to note that pertussis is a serious illness that requires medical attention, and you should consult with a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for pertussis

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus serpyllum creams, which contain thymol, a compound found in the herb thyme, have been studied for their potential to help alleviate symptoms of pertussis, also known as whooping cough. The thymol in Thymus serpyllum creams has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce the severity of the infection and shorten its duration. Thymol has been shown to inhibit the growth of Bordetella pertussis, the bacteria that causes pertussis, and to reduce inflammation in the airways, making it easier to breathe. If you suspect you or your child has pertussis, it's essential to consult a healthcare professional for proper diagnosis and treatment. In the meantime, you can try applying Thymus serpyllum creams to the affected area, such as the chest or back, to help soothe and calm the skin. You can also try inhaling the vapor from the cream, by gently heating it and inhaling the steam, to help loosen mucus and reduce congestion. However, do not use Thymus serpyllum creams as a replacement for medical treatment, but rather as a complementary therapy to help manage symptoms. Taking action with Thymus serpyllum creams may lead to a reduction in coughing fits, fever, and overall discomfort. Some people may experience relief from congestion and breathing difficulties, making it easier to rest and recover. However, it's crucial to note that Thymus serpyllum creams may not cure pertussis, and treatment should always be supervised by a healthcare professional.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus serpyllum herbal creams for pertussis

intro_desc: Infants are very young and their bodies are still growing. They have a weak immune system, which makes them more likely to catch illnesses like pertussis, also known as whooping cough. When an infant gets pertussis, it can be very serious. They might start coughing a lot, and this can make it hard for them to breathe and eat. They might also get very tired and have trouble sleeping. In severe cases, infants with pertussis can even stop breathing, which is a life-threatening situation. This can be very scary for parents and caregivers, who want to help their little ones feel better.  Infants with pertussis often need to go to the hospital, where they might get oxygen therapy or other treatments to help them breathe. They might also need antibiotics to help fight off the bacteria that's causing the illness. But even with treatment, infants with pertussis can still have a tough time. They might get very irritable, and have trouble feeding or sleeping. This can be a stressful time for parents, who want to do everything they can to help their baby feel better.  One thing that some parents are turning to is herbal creams. These creams are made from natural ingredients that can help soothe and calm the skin. When an infant has pertussis, the skin can get very dry and irritated from all the coughing. The herbal creams can help moisturize and calm the skin, which can make the infant feel more comfortable. They can also help reduce inflammation and promote healing. Some parents are finding that these creams can be a helpful addition to traditional treatments, and can help their baby feel more relaxed and comfortable while they recover from pertussis.
